title: size_t used in LOG format without portable specifier
description:
Locations:
- firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lichen_l2.c:285,300,322,331,659,660,737,787
- firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lora_l2.c:212,475,504

Multiple LOG_* calls use %zu for size_t, which is correct C99, but Zephyr logging may not support %zu on all platforms. Zephyr recommends casting to unsigned long and using %lu for maximum portability.

What goes wrong: On platforms where size_t is different width than printf expects for %zu (some embedded toolchains), log output would show garbage values, hindering debugging.

Note: Modern Zephyr (3.x+) with printk supports %zu, but older versions may not. Check Zephyr version compatibility.
close_reason: Not a bug: Zephyr 3.7.0 with default cbprintf (not NANO mode) fully supports %zu format specifier for size_t. The original concern about older toolchains not supporting %zu is not applicable to this project's configuration. Current code is correct C99 and works with the configured Zephyr version. No changes needed.
